candidate_llm_application: IFA-256-RUBRIC — structured judge LLM with 256-cell fixed rubric catalog indexed by 8-bit binary signature. Judge runs 8 specialized binary classifiers on candidate response (hallucination, correctness, format, refusal-shift, sycophancy, bias, harmful, scoped); 8-bit vector S=(c_1..c_8) indexes into 256-cell lookup table R[S] with structured verdict + rationale template + recommended remediation.
rule: hit iff kw_count>=2 OR sem_score>=0.7 OR func_score>=0.7; total_hits>=1 => FAIL
content_words: opon ifa wooden tray ese verse
babalawo binary palm-nut signature LLM judge

results.title: Rubric-Based LLM Judge Methodologies — per-criterion judge + criterion-level reward
results.rationale: Rubric-Based LLM Judge Methodologies IS the per-criterion judge + criterion-level reward paradigm — directly the candidate's 8-binary-criteria + per-criterion verdict template substrate. The candidate's 8 binary classifiers (hallucination/correctness/format/refusal-shift/sycophancy/bias/harmful/scoped) with cell-specific rationale template IS the per-criterion-judge with per-criterion-reward design with eight discrete criteria. Functional-twin on the per-criterion-rubric-judge substrate; semantic-twin on the rubric-based-evaluation framing. Keyword overlap on '8-bit binary trace structured judge signature' (kw_count=1).

results.title: Autorubric 2603.00077 — unified rubric + criterion-vector lookup
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN on the criterion-vector-lookup paradigm. Autorubric 2603.00077 IS the unified rubric with criterion-vector-lookup design — directly the candidate's 8-bit vector S=(c_1..c_8) indexing into 256-cell lookup table R[S] verbatim. The candidate's mechanism (run K binary classifiers, assemble criterion vector, lookup verdict cell) IS Autorubric's criterion-vector-lookup with K=8. Functional-twin AND semantic-twin on the criterion-vector-lookup-rubric substrate. The Yoruba/Ifa/odu/babalawo/opon-ifa cultural framing supplies only narrative for the criterion-vector-to-cell-lookup design Autorubric specifies. Keyword overlap on '256-cell fixed verdict-lookup rubric catalog' framing (kw_count=1).

results.title: Rubric-Grounded RL 2605.08061 — multi-dim structured judge rewards
results.rationale: Rubric-Grounded RL IS the multi-dimensional structured-judge reward substrate the candidate's 8-binary-criteria + structured verdict instantiates. The candidate's 8-dim binary criterion vector with cell-specific verdict IS Rubric-Grounded RL's multi-dim structured-judge-reward decomposed into 8 binary dimensions with a discrete 256-cell verdict table. Functional-twin on the multi-dim-structured-judge substrate; semantic-twin on the rubric-grounded-evaluation framing.

results.title: Langfuse LLM-as-a-Judge — fixed catalog (Hallucination, Toxicity, Helpfulness, ...)
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN on the fixed-catalog-of-judge-criteria paradigm. Langfuse Catalog IS the fixed-criterion-catalog design (Hallucination, Toxicity, Helpfulness, ...) — directly the candidate's fixed 8-criterion catalog (hallucination, correctness, format, refusal-shift, sycophancy, bias, harmful, scoped) verbatim. The candidate's first stage (run K specialized classifiers each detecting one canonical issue type from a fixed catalog) IS Langfuse's catalog-of-evaluators verbatim. Keyword overlap on '256-cell fixed verdict-lookup rubric catalog' (kw_count=1). Functional-twin AND semantic-twin on the fixed-catalog-judge-criteria substrate.

results.title: EvidentlyAI LLM-as-Judge — criterion-level binary/categorical scoring
results.rationale: EvidentlyAI LLM-as-Judge IS the criterion-level binary/categorical-scoring design — directly the candidate's c_i in {0,1} per-criterion binary classification verbatim. The candidate's 8 binary criteria scoring IS EvidentlyAI's criterion-level binary-categorical-judge with 8 dimensions. Functional-twin on the criterion-level-binary-scoring substrate; semantic-twin on the binary-categorical-evaluation framing.

results.title: LLMs Tabular Data Survey 2402.17944 — hierarchical binary decision-tree
results.rationale: LLMs Tabular Data Survey 2402.17944 catalogs the hierarchical-binary-decision-tree paradigm — directly the candidate's 8-bit binary signature indexing into 256-cell verdict table (2^8 leaf nodes of a depth-8 binary decision tree). The candidate's 8-bit-vector-to-256-cell-lookup IS a flat representation of a depth-8 binary decision tree the survey documents. Functional-twin on the hierarchical-binary-classifier substrate; semantic-twin on the binary-decision-tree framing.

results.title: Multiclass Text Classification LLM Perficient — multi-class taxonomy classification
results.rationale: Multiclass Text Classification Perficient IS the multi-class taxonomy-classification substrate — directly the candidate's 256-cell verdict classification (a 256-class taxonomy classification). The candidate's final routing (route candidate response to one of 256 verdict cells) IS Perficient's multi-class taxonomy-classification with 256 classes assembled from a Cartesian product of 8 binary criteria. Functional-twin on the multi-class-taxonomy-routing substrate; semantic-twin on the multi-class-classification framing.

results.title: Lamini AI LLM-Classifier — category-catalog routing
results.rationale: Lamini AI LLM-Classifier IS the category-catalog-routing substrate — directly the candidate's R[S] lookup-table routing to one of 256 verdict cells. The candidate's catalog R = {R_1..R_256} with structured verdict + rationale template per cell IS Lamini's category-catalog-routing with 256 categories and cell-specific templates. Functional-twin on the category-catalog-routing substrate; semantic-twin on the catalog-based-classification framing.

reason: 8 of 8 results trigger hit; all via semantic + functional channels (kw_hits = 0 due to source-side keywords being culturally specific Yoruba terms not overlapping with prior-art literature). Maximum semantic 0.92 and maximum functional 0.90 on Autorubric 2603.00077 — a near-verbatim criterion-vector-lookup match to the candidate's 8-bit-vector indexing into 256-cell R[S] table. The candidate decomposes exhaustively across four prior-art clusters: (cluster A — criterion-vector-lookup rubric) Autorubric 2603.00077 (sem 0.92, func 0.90) IS the unified rubric + criterion-vector-lookup design verbatim; Rubric-Grounded RL 2605.08061 (sem 0.86, func 0.84) supplies multi-dim structured-judge-reward substrate. (cluster B — fixed-criteria-catalog judge) Langfuse LLM-as-a-Judge (sem 0.88, func 0.86) IS the fixed-criterion-catalog design (Hallucination, Toxicity, Helpfulness) verbatim — directly matching the candidate's 8-criterion fixed catalog; Rubric-Based LLM Judge Methodologies Medium 2026 (sem 0.88, func 0.86) supplies per-criterion-judge + criterion-level-reward substrate; EvidentlyAI LLM-as-Judge (sem 0.84, func 0.82) supplies criterion-level binary/categorical-scoring substrate. (cluster C — hierarchical binary decision tree / 2^K classification) LLMs Tabular Data Survey 2402.17944 (sem 0.74, func 0.72) supplies the hierarchical-binary-decision-tree paradigm the candidate's 8-bit-signature-to-256-cell IS a flat representation of. (cluster D — multi-class taxonomy + category-catalog routing) Multiclass Text Classification Perficient (sem 0.78, func 0.74) supplies multi-class taxonomy-classification substrate; Lamini AI LLM-Classifier (sem 0.76, func 0.72) supplies category-catalog-routing substrate. The three claimed contributions — (a) 8-bit specific binary classifier vector, (b) 256-cell fixed lookup-table, (c) cell-specific verdict template — are anticipated cluster-by-cluster: (a) by Langfuse's fixed-criterion-catalog (8 named criteria) + EvidentlyAI's criterion-level-binary-scoring; (b) by Autorubric's criterion-vector-lookup design verbatim + Lamini's category-catalog-routing; (c) by Rubric-Based LLM Judge Methodologies' per-criterion-rationale + Rubric-Grounded RL's structured-multi-dim verdicts. The candidate explicitly concedes in its own novelty_claim: '256-class taxonomies are mainstream classification'. The Yoruba/Ifa/odu/babalawo/opon-ifa/256-odu-corpus cultural framing supplies only narrative for the K-binary-criteria + 2^K-cell-lookup + per-cell-rationale-template design; the underlying mechanism is fully covered. total_hits = 8 >> 1, distinct_effect_clusters = 4 >> 1, candidate FAILS.
recommendation: REJECT candidate. IFA-256-RUBRIC is fully anticipated by the conjunction Autorubric 2603.00077 + Rubric-Based LLM Judge Methodologies + Langfuse LLM-as-a-Judge + Rubric-Grounded RL 2605.08061 + EvidentlyAI LLM-as-Judge + LLMs Tabular Data Survey 2402.17944 + Multiclass Text Classification Perficient + Lamini AI LLM-Classifier. Specifically: (1) the 8-bit binary classifier vector S=(c_1..c_8) IS Langfuse's fixed-criterion-catalog (8 named binary judge criteria) + EvidentlyAI's criterion-level binary-scoring; (2) the 256-cell fixed lookup-table R[S] IS Autorubric's criterion-vector-lookup design verbatim + Lamini's category-catalog-routing with 256 categories; (3) the cell-specific verdict + rationale template + remediation IS Rubric-Based LLM Judge Methodologies' per-criterion-rationale + Rubric-Grounded RL's multi-dim structured-judge-reward; (4) the 2^8 = 256 cell structure IS LLMs Tabular Data Survey's hierarchical-binary-decision-tree paradigm flat-tabulated; (5) the 8 specific criteria (hallucination, correctness, format, refusal-shift, sycophancy, bias, harmful, scoped) IS the Langfuse + EvidentlyAI mainstream judge-criterion-catalog. To salvage, the candidate would need a feature uniquely supplied by the Ifa-divination source not anticipated by these clusters, e.g.: (a) a 16-main-x-16-derivative two-level hierarchical structure operationalized with formal information-theoretic decomposition advantage over flat 256-cell lookup — beyond Autorubric's criterion-vector design; (b) an Ese-verse-corpus-per-cell operationalized as a retrieval-augmented verdict-generation with formal grounding-density lower bound per cell — beyond ordinary rationale-template attachment; (c) a 4-vertical-pair-2x4-matrix encoding operationalized as a structured 2-axis criterion decomposition (e.g., factuality-axis x style-axis) with provable orthogonality — beyond a flat 8-bit vector indistinguishable from Autorubric's vector; (d) a babalawo-binary-casting operationalized as a randomized-classifier-ensemble protocol with formal disagreement-resolution beyond majority-vote. As stated, IFA-256-RUBRIC is in the saturated region documented by Autorubric + Langfuse + Rubric-Based LLM Judge Methodologies + Rubric-Grounded RL + EvidentlyAI + LLMs Tabular Survey + Multiclass Classification Perficient + Lamini Classifier, with each of the three claimed contributions anticipated by at least two prior-art clusters, and the candidate's own novelty statement explicitly acknowledges '256-class taxonomies are mainstream classification'.
